raw_output:
A suitable way to complete the sentence is:

**“The engineer explained the delay that the equipment failure caused.”** 

Here **“the equipment failure”** fills the blank as the noun phrase that caused the delay, and the relative clause **“that … caused”** correctly attaches it to *delay*. (Any other specific cause—e.g., “the redesign,” “the software bug,” “he” (if referring to the engineer himself), etc.—could be inserted in the same position.)
